# OMG UML 2.5.1 - Cite-Only Pointer

This document is a cite-only pointer. It names the Unified Modeling Language (UML) version 2.5.1 specification published by the Object Management Group (OMG) and the BRD Builder's posture toward it; it does not redistribute the specification's text, notation glyph descriptions, metamodel diagrams, or normative tables.

## What UML 2.5.1 Is

UML 2.5.1 is the OMG standard notation for object-oriented modeling. It defines a family of diagram types covering structure (class, component, deployment), behavior (state machine, activity, use case), and interaction (sequence, communication, timing). The BRD Builder uses UML 2.5.1 whenever a requirement is best expressed as a structural, behavioral, or interaction model that is not a business process (BPMN) or a decision (DMN).

The BRD Builder references UML 2.5.1 in two ways:

* As the notation family selected by the [`process-modeling`](../SKILL.md#notation-selection) decision when a requirement needs a class, component, sequence, state, or use-case diagram.
* As the upstream source of all syntactic and semantic detail. The BRD Builder never reproduces UML's glyphs, metamodel, or notation semantics in this repository; readers are pointed at the OMG specification for any normative content.

## Why Cite-Only

The UML 2.5.1 specification is published by the OMG under terms that permit reference and citation but not unrestricted redistribution. Embedding the specification's text, the notation glyph descriptions, or the metamodel diagrams is therefore out of scope for this repository.

The BRD Builder's posture is:

* Reference UML 2.5.1 by name and version when explaining why a diagram uses classes, components, sequences, states, or use cases.
* Do not paraphrase the specification's normative text. Any wording in this repository that describes UML behavior is original Microsoft content and is kept brief and orientational.
* When a stakeholder needs the canonical definition of a construct, link them to the OMG catalog entry below.

## Upstream Source

[https://www.omg.org/spec/UML/2.5.1/](https://www.omg.org/spec/UML/2.5.1/) (accessed 2026-05-25) - OMG specification page, where the current revision, formal documents, and license terms are obtained.
